rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether transfer of venue to San Mateo County should be approved. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALIREZA TABESH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/23/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been provided. 2) No proposed orders have been provided. 3) Allegations regarding notice required under Probate Code sections 9201 & 9202 must be provided. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions requiring notice of proposed action but without court supervision? If so, all information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be provided. 5) Is the estate property listed on the inventory and appraisal the decedent's separate property or one-half of community property? Probate Code section 8850(c). 6) No election by the surviving spouse is on file. Probate Code section 13502. As the deadline for making the election has passed, an ex parte petition for allowance of a late election must be filed if the surviving spouse seeks to probate any part of the decedent's interest passing to her. The election must include a statement required by LRSF 14.67(L)(5). 7) The date of death set forth in paragraph 1 of the petition is incorrect.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACQUELINE BADENHAUSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/09/2020. Petitioner needs to file a Petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of status report, pay a filing fee, and provide a proposed order and courtesy copy of the petition no later than 10/26/2020 at 4pm to the Examiner in room 202. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAKE PHELPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/23/2020. Attorney Elizabeth Read filed documents late. Supplements, amendments, or other papers addressing issues raised by an Examiner and other responsive papers must be filed seven (7) court days before the hearing, and endorsed-filed courtesy copies must be received by the Examiner no later than five (5) Court days before the hearing. [LRSF 14.6 C.] Attorney Elizabeth Read has not provided Examiner with the required courtesy copy of the 10/05/2020 Supplemental Memorandum, 10/05/2020 Declaration, 10/05/2020 Request for Judicial Notice, 10/13/2020 Memorandum and Objections, and 10/13/2020 Declaration. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 no later than 4pm on 11/09/2020 at the Superior Court. [LRSF 2.7, 14.6]. Attorney Joseph Barton has not filed notice of the 10/07/2020 Amendment to Amended Spousal Property Petition and has not filed notice of the 10/07/2020 First Amendded Petition to Establish Status as Surviving Putative Spouse. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARY MARGARET ISHAM REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/16/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The beginning assets do not include tangible personal items. Have they been distributed? The proof of service does not show notice was given to some beneficiaries of the tangible personal items. 2) Schedule C shows the $31,875 broker commission was paid to Side, Inc., but the order confirming the sale states the commission was payable to Elevation Real Estate. 3) Are all the individual beneficiaries adults? If there are any minor beneficiaries, the person(s) having legal custody of and residing with the minors must be identified and given notice. CRC 7.51(d). 4) Schedule J needs to specify the names of the persons receiving distributions on behalf of Davin Fine, Kai Lyon-Wiess, and Arianna Thomasburger. Notice must also be given to them. 5) Schedule J does not address how an unused portion of the reserve should be distributed. 6) Should Article 4 of the trust instrument apply to determine the disposition of the cash gift to Unidosporpuertorico.com? 7) Paragraph 9 of the petition appears to be requesting modification of the trust based on the cypress doctrine, but the caption of the petition does not include this relief.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY CHRISTINE OWENSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine how to proceed, although the date of death has not been corrected in an amendment to the petition or an amended petition. Please review the 6th amended general order on the court's website re the strong preference for all attorneys and parties to appear remotely as a result of the COVID-19 emergency and for information on appearing by CourtCall or via Zoom. (A 7th amended general order is imminent. Parties should check the Court's website closer to the hearing date for further instructions regarding remote appearance.) For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN HENRY MCCOY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/09/2020. The second supplement (filed 10/13/2020) does not fully address CRC 7.52 (approximate date when Christopher McCoy was last known to reside at the address listed in the petition, inquiry of his friends, acquaintances, and employers, and search of the real and personal property indexes). Also, has the notice mailed to the last known address been returned as undeliverable? What websites did the petitioner use for the search?
